extensionDescription.message: Analyze DMARC aggregate and forensic reports locally within Thunderbird. Identify threats, validate your email authentication, and take action.

noFolderNotice.message: No DMARC folders configured. Please open Settings to select your report folders, or create folders matching the naming convention (e.g. DMARC/Aggregate, DMARC/Forensic) for auto-detection.
noFolderNotice.description: Notice when no folders are set

statusErrors.description: Error count label in status bar

statusCached.description: Label indicating results are from cache

deliveredFail.description: Disposition: delivered but authentication failed — dangerous if p=none

dkimSpfPass.description: Card label: both DKIM and SPF passed

colClassification.description: Table column: IP range classification tag

colOverrideType.description: Table column: policy override reason type

labelArFolder.description: Settings label for aggregate report folder

labelFrFolder.description: Settings label for forensic report folder

hintArFolder.message: Select the IMAP folder where DMARC aggregate reports are delivered.
hintArFolder.description: Hint text for aggregate report folder selection

hintFrFolder.message: Select the IMAP folder where DMARC forensic reports are delivered. Optional.
hintFrFolder.description: Hint text for forensic report folder selection

tagLegitimate.description: IP classification: all pass, all delivered

tagLegitimateDesc.message: All messages passed authentication and were delivered normally. This is a legitimate sending source.

tagBlocked.description: IP classification: all fail, all rejected

tagBlockedDesc.message: All messages failed authentication and were rejected. Your DMARC policy is working correctly against this source.

tagMisconfiguredDesc.message: Some messages passed but others were rejected or quarantined. Check SPF/DKIM configuration for this sending source.

tagThreat.description: IP classification: auth fail but delivered

tagThreatDesc.message: Authentication failed but messages were delivered anyway. This source may be spoofing your domain. Consider setting p=reject.

healthUnderAttack.description: Domain health: attacks are being blocked

healthNeedsAttention.description: Domain health: unblocked auth failures or weak policy

issueNoAttachmentDetail.message: No DMARC report attachment found in this message
issueNoAttachmentDetail.description: Detail text for no attachment issue

privacyNote.message: All processing is local. No external requests.

warnDateRangeBegin.message: Report date range begin is missing

warnDateRangeEnd.message: Report date range end is missing

warnRecordSourceIp.message: Source IP is missing in record

warnRecordCount.message: Message count is zero in record

warnRecordDkim.message: DKIM evaluation result is missing in record

warnRecordSpf.message: SPF evaluation result is missing in record

warnRecordAuthResults.message: No DKIM or SPF auth_results in record

warnRecordHeaderFrom.message: header_from identifier is missing in record

warnFrDomain.message: Reported domain could not be determined

warnFrArrivalDate.message: Arrival date could not be determined
